in_distribution_eval.note: Fresh tasks drawn from the training prior. The model has learned to do in-context binary classification on the synthetic distribution.

zero_shot_breast_cancer.note: Zero-shot in-context classification. PFN has never seen the dataset; it predicts purely from the 48-sample context in each of 30 bootstrap passes (probabilities averaged per test point). LogReg fits the full 426-sample train fold.

notes: Headline: zero-shot 74.5% accuracy / 0.80 AUC on a real medical dataset, training only on synthetic random-hyperplane tasks.

Still 24 percentage points behind LogReg (which fits on the same labelled data). Closing that gap is what richer priors (BNN-generated tasks, SCM mixtures — like TabPFN's actual prior set) buy you. Out of scope for this study.

Critical pipeline bug fixed during this study: the training loop was passing seed=base+step to sample_batch, which made consecutive steps share batch_size-1 seeds. The model was overfitting to the last ~32 batches and forgetting earlier ones. With seed=base+step*batch_size every task is seen exactly once and the model actually learns to generalize. Same fix benefits every prior in the catalog.
